info.name: Brello HRMS API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Brello HRMS multi-tenant, multi-app backend.

## Setup
1. Set the `base_url` variable to your server address (default: `http://localhost:8000`)
2. Start with the **Login** request to get tokens
3. Access token is auto-saved to collection variables. Refresh token is delivered as an HttpOnly cookie (`refresh_token`).

## Authentication Flow
1. Login → saves `access_token`, refresh token is set as HttpOnly cookie
2. Use authenticated endpoints (access token is auto-injected)
3. Switch App → saves new `access_token`
4. Refresh → browser/Postman sends cookie automatically, rotates both tokens

item.name: Onboarding
item.description: Flow from lead registration to company setup

request.description: Register as a new lead. Requires a valid `plan_id`.

exec: console.log('✅ Lead registered. Check console for OTP successful.');

request.description: Verify lead email using OTP. Default dev OTP is `123456`.

exec: console.log('✅ Access token and IDs saved.');

request.description: Login initially to get the `user_id` for company setup.
The final step of the onboarding flow. Links the user to a new organization.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication and authorization endpoints

exec: console.log('✅ Access token saved. Refresh token is in HttpOnly cookie.');

request.description:
Authenticate with email and password.

**Auto-saves:** `access_token`, `user_id`, `enterprise_id`, `organization_id`, `default_app_id` to collection variables.

**Refresh token:** Delivered as an HttpOnly cookie (`refresh_token`). Postman stores it automatically in the cookie jar.

exec: // Store the email from request body so Verify OTP can reuse it.
console.log('📧 OTP login email staged:', body.email);
pm.test('Send OTP responded with 204 No Content', function () {
console.log('✅ OTP sent. Check server console for the OTP in dev mode.');
console.log('📌 Next step: run "Login - Verify OTP" with the OTP from your email/console.');

request.description:
Initiate OTP-based login. Sends a 6-digit OTP to the provided email.

**Returns:** 204 No Content on success.

**Auto-stages:** `otp_login_email` collection variable, so the follow-up **Login - Verify OTP** request is pre-filled.

**In dev mode:** The OTP is printed to the server console.

exec: // Automatically fill email from the variable set by 'Login - Send OTP'.
console.log('📧 Email auto-filled from otp_login_email:', stagedEmail);
console.warn('⚠️  otp_login_email not set. Run "Login - Send OTP" first, or fill email manually.');
pm.test('Verify OTP responded with 200 OK', function () {
// Clean up the staging variable after successful login.
console.log('✅ OTP login successful. Access token saved.');
console.log('✅ Refresh token delivered as HttpOnly cookie.');
console.error('❌ OTP verification failed:', errorBody.message || pm.response.status);

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent to the email and complete the OTP-based login.

**Email is auto-filled** from the `otp_login_email` variable set by **Login - Send OTP**. You only need to paste the OTP.

**Auto-saves on success:** `access_token`, `user_id`, `enterprise_id`, `organization_id`, `default_app_id`.

**Refresh token:** Delivered as HttpOnly cookie — stored automatically by Postman's cookie jar.

**OTP rules:** 6 digits, max 5 attempts, expires in 10 minutes.

Switch to a different application. Provide the target `appId` (get available apps from the Login response).

**Auto-saves:** New `access_token` scoped to the target app.

exec: console.log('✅ Access token rotated. Refresh token cookie also rotated.');

request.description:
Refresh the access token using the HttpOnly cookie.

**No Authorization header needed.** The refresh token is sent automatically as a cookie by the browser (or Postman).

**Auto-saves:** New `access_token` to collection variable. New refresh token cookie is set automatically (token rotation).
Returns a flat list of all granted permissions for the logged-in user in the current app context. Includes `module_code` and `action_code` for each permission.
Invalidate the current session and clear the refresh token cookie. Returns 204 No Content.

exec: console.log('✅ Logged out. Access token cleared.');

request.description:
Change the authenticated user's password.

**Side effect:** All active sessions are invalidated (user must re-login).

**Password rules:** Min 8 chars, must contain: uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character.

exec: console.log('✅ Password updated. All sessions invalidated — please re-login.');

request.description:
Request a password reset OTP. Always returns 204 (even if email doesn't exist, for security).

**In dev mode:** Check the server console for the OTP log.

exec: console.log('✅ Password reset OTP sent. Check server console for OTP successful.');

request.description:
Verify the OTP received via email and set a new password.

**OTP rules:** Exactly 6 digits, max 5 attempts, expires in 10 minutes.

exec: console.log('✅ Password reset successful. Please re-login successful.');

request.description:
Unified endpoint to resend an OTP for any purpose (LOGIN, RESET_PASSWORD, PLATFORM_ADMIN_LOGIN, etc).

**Requirement:** An initial OTP request must have already been made for this email and purpose.

exec: console.log('✅ OTP resent. Check server console for OTP successful.');

request.description: Initiates the registration flow for a new Platform Admin by securely masking their initial creation and sending an OTP to tech@brello.co.in.

exec: console.log('✅ Platform Admin registration OTP sent successful.');

request.description: Verifies the OTP and activates the Platform Admin account.

exec: console.log('✅ Platform Admin account activated successful.');

request.description: Initiates the login flow for an existing Platform Admin by validating credentials and sending an OTP to their email.

exec: console.log('✅ Platform Admin login OTP sent. Check console successful.');
console.log('✅ Access token saved. Refresh token in HttpOnly cookie.');

request.description:
Verifies the login OTP and formally completes authentication by returning tokens.

**Auto-saves:** `access_token`, `user_id` to collection variables.

**Refresh token:** Delivered as an HttpOnly cookie (`refresh_token`). Postman stores it automatically.

request.description: Upload the binary file. In Postman, go to Body -> form-data -> Hover over 'file' key and change type to 'File' -> Select your PDF.

request.description: Final step to transition employee from DRAFT to INVITED status.

request.description: Admin action to transition employee from INVITED to ACTIVE. Updates both employee_status and user status, enabling the employee to log in.
List employees with pagination, search, and filters.
Get a simplified list of employees (label/value) for dropdowns, scoped to the current organization.
Get complete employee aggregate data.

Create a new application.

**Auto-saves:** `app_id` to collection variables.

`priority` determines the default app on login (lower = higher priority).
List all registered applications, sorted by priority.
Get a single app by UUID.
Partially update an app.
Delete an app. Returns 204 No Content.

item.name: RBAC
item.description: Role-Based Access Control — roles, user-role assignments, and menu

Create a new role scoped to an app.

**Auto-saves:** `role_id` to collection variables.
List all roles with their associated app.
Get a single role by UUID.
Get all roles belonging to a specific app.
Partially update a role.
Delete a role. Returns 204 No Content.

Assign a role to a user in a specific organization.

**This is required before the user can login!**

exec: console.log('✅ Role assigned to user successful.');

request.description: List all user-role assignments.
Get all role assignments for a specific user.
Remove a role assignment. Returns 204 No Content.

Returns the authenticated user's accessible module tree for the current app.

The response is a hierarchical tree where:
- Each node has `id`, `name`, `wbs_code`, `actions[]`, and `children[]`
- Only modules the user has permission to access are included
- Actions are filtered by both role permissions AND plan restrictions
- Tree is sorted by WBS code at each level

item.name: Lead
item.description: Public lead registration and OTP verification

request.description:
Register a new lead. Sends a verification OTP to the provided email.

**No authentication required.**

**In dev mode:** Check the server console for the OTP log.

**Validation:** email (unique), first_name (min 2), last_name (min 2), phone (E.164), password (strong), source (must be 'website').

exec: console.log('✅ Lead registered. Check server console for OTP successful.');

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent during lead registration.

**On success:**
- Lead is marked as verified
- A User record is created from the lead details (first_name, last_name, email, phone, password_hash)
- Both operations are wrapped in a transaction — if anything fails, everything rolls back.

**OTP rules:** Exactly 6 digits, max 5 attempts, expires in 10 minutes.

exec: console.log('✅ Lead verified. user_id saved:', body.data.user_id);

item.name: Role
item.description: Role management — CRUD for platform and organization roles

request.description:
Create a new role. Only platform admins can set `is_system_defined: true`.

**Auto-saves:** `role_id` to collection variables.

Standardized listing of all roles.

**Features:**
- Pagination, Search, and Sorting
- Filter by `is_system_role`
- Auto-scoped to organization/enterprise.
Get roles scoped to the given organization & enterprise, plus all system-defined roles. Both query params are required UUIDs.
Get a single role by its UUID.
Partially update a role. All fields are optional. Only platform admins can modify system-defined roles.
Soft delete a role. Returns 204 No Content. Only platform admins can delete system-defined roles.

item.name: Department
item.description: Department management within organizations

request.description: Create a new department. The organization is resolved from the authenticated user's profile.

Standardized listing of all departments in the user's organization.

**Features:**
- Pagination, Search, and Sorting
- Includes `memberAvatars` (first 3 employees in the department).
Fetch details of a single department by its ID.
Update department details. All fields are optional.
Soft delete a department. Sets status to INACTIVE and is_deleted to true.
Returns a paginated list of all projects across all clients for the user's organization.

exec: console.log('✅ Team assigned to project successful.');

request.description: Returns a detailed list of team members assigned to the project, including roles and user profiles.
Returns a list of all contract documents uploaded for the project.
Removes a single team member from the project.
Removes a contract from the project and deletes the associated document.

item.name: Documents
item.description: Document management and storage endpoints

item.name: Attendance Config
item.description: Attendance configuration module: Shifts, Weekly Offs, Attendance Rules, Rule Assignments, and Geo-Fencing Validation.

request.description:
Create a new work shift.

Validations:
- end_time > start_time
- auto_checkout_time >= end_time
- full_day_hours > half_day_hours

Auto-saves: `shift_id`
List all shifts for the organization with pagination.
Partial update of a shift.

Change shift status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE).

Returns 409 CONFLICT if the shift is used in active attendance rules.

Create a weekly off pattern.

Validations:
- At least 1 day required
- Max 7 days
- No duplicate days

Auto-saves: `weekly_off_id`
List all weekly off patterns for the organization.
Partial update of a weekly off pattern.

Change weekly off status.

Returns 409 CONFLICT if linked to active attendance rules.

Create an attendance rule linking a shift and weekly off.

Validations:
- shift_id must exist
- weekly_off_id must exist
- full_day_hours > half_day_hours
- overtime_after_hours >= full_day_hours
- If require_geo_fencing=true, geo_fence is required

Auto-saves: `attendance_rule_id`
Create an attendance rule without geo-fencing (for remote teams).
List all attendance rules with joined shift and weekly off names.
Partial update of an attendance rule.
Change attendance rule status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE).

Assign an attendance rule to departments.

Behavior:
- Assigns rule to all employees in the departments
- Overwrites any previous department-level rule assignment for the same department

Assign an attendance rule directly to employees.

Critical: Employee-level assignment OVERRIDES department-level assignment.
List all assignments (department + employee) for an attendance rule.

Validate if an employee's location is within the geo-fence radius.

Uses Haversine formula for distance calculation.

Returns:
- is_within_radius: boolean
- distance: number (meters)

Errors:
- 404 NOT_FOUND: No rule assigned to employee
- 422 INVALID_STATE: Geo-fencing not enabled for the rule

item.name: Payroll
item.description: Payroll configuration module: Settings, Component Master, PF Config, Salary Templates, Employee Salary, Change Propagation, and Dry Run Simulation.

request.description:
Create or update the organization's payroll cycle settings. financial_end_month and financial_year_label are auto-derived from financial_start_month.

payout_type options: last_working_day | first_working_day | custom
attendance_cutoff_type options: days_before_month_end | fixed_date
financial_start_month options: jan | feb | mar | apr | may | jun | jul | aug | sep | oct | nov | dec
Get current payroll settings for the organization.

Returns all active components sorted by calculation_priority. Auto-seeds the 5 default components on first call:
- CTC (virtual root, priority 0)
- Basic Salary (50% of CTC, priority 1)
- PF Employee (12% of Basic, priority 2)
- PF Employer (12% of Basic, priority 3)
- Special Allowance (residual, priority 99)

Creates a custom salary component.

component_type: earning | deduction | bonus
category: fixed | variable | statutory
calculation_type: fixed | percentage | residual

Rules:
- calculate_from must be the UUID of another component (use Basic Salary's UUID for % of Basic)
- is_residual: only ONE allowed per org
- Default components cannot be created via this endpoint

Auto-saves: payroll_component_id
Updates a component. For default components (is_default: true), only value and calculation_priority can be changed.

Deletes a custom component.

Fails if:
- Component is a default (is_default: true)
- Component is used in any salary template

Creates a new versioned PF config. The current active config is automatically closed (effective_to = new effective_from − 1 day).

Note: effective_from must be after the current active config's effective_from date.
Note: Wage ceiling has been removed from this version.
Returns the currently active PF config (where effective_to is null).
Returns all PF config versions ordered by effective_from DESC.

Creates a salary template. CTC, Basic Salary, and Special Allowance are auto-included even if not listed.

Validations:
- No circular component dependencies (DAG check)
- Each component's dependency must be included in the template
- sort_order of a dependent must be higher than its base component

Auto-saves: salary_template_id
Returns all salary templates for the organization with their components.
Returns a single template with full component and base_component relations.

Simulates the full salary breakdown against a template and CTC without saving anything. Uses the same calculation engine as actual assignment.

Returns gross, deductions_total, net, employer_contribution, earnings[], deductions[], warnings[], and metadata.

Assigns a salary template to an employee. Creates version 1 or increments to the next version. Previous version is automatically closed with effective_to = effective_from − 1 day.

Stores a full component snapshot — changes to the template after assignment do NOT affect the employee.
Assigns the same template and CTC to multiple employees at once. Returns { assigned: N, failed: [] } — failed contains user IDs that could not be processed.
Paginated list of employees with name, employee code, and department.
Returns the active salary version with full component breakdown including version number and effective date.
Returns all past salary versions for the employee ordered by version number descending.

Creates a new salary version with updated component values. The residual component (Special Allowance) is recalculated automatically. Always creates a new version — never mutates history.

Note: use component_name (not code) to identify components.
Returns the number of salary templates and active employee salaries that contain this component. Use this before applying propagation to understand the blast radius.

Pushes an updated component value to selected employees by creating new salary versions.

scope options:
- future_only: update master component only, no employee impact (safe default)
- selected_employees: create new salary versions for selected employees

effective_from must not be in the past.

Returns { updated: N, skipped: N }.

item.name: Leave Configuration
item.description: Endpoints for managing leave types, rules, and configurations.

exec: console.log('✅ Leave Config ID saved:', response.data.id);

request.description: Create a new draft leave configuration. A draft cannot be used for leave applications until activated.
Fetch details of a specific leave configuration including its types and rules.
Update an existing leave configuration. You can update the year start month, total leaves, leave types, and rules.
Activate a leave configuration. This will deactivate the current active configuration and make this one active. This action is irreversible.

item.name: Leave Balance
item.description: Per-employee leave balance ledger: initialize, read, adjust, recompute. Self-service `GET /me` requires only JWT; HR endpoints require `LEAVE_MGMT.{view|create|update}` permission.

request.description: Self-service. Returns the caller's balance for the requested year. LWP rows are always present with `is_unlimited: true` and null balance fields.
HR / manager view. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.view`.
Creates one balance row per LeaveType in the active LeaveConfig. Idempotent — second call returns 409 ALREADY_INITIALIZED. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.create`.

Initializes balances for all employees in scope. `scope`: `ORGANIZATION` | `DEPARTMENT` (with `department_ids[]`) | `EMPLOYEES` (with `employee_ids[]`). Already-initialized employees are skipped, not failed. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.create`.

query.description: Free-text on employee name OR employee code (>= 2 chars)

request.description: Paginated HR list with filters. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.view`.
Single balance row. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.view`.
Immutable audit trail of credits/debits for a balance row. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.view`.
Manual credit/debit. `days` must be in 0.5 increments. Reason is mandatory. Returns 422 UNLIMITED_TYPE_NOT_ADJUSTABLE for LWP. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.update`.
Self-heal: recomputes used/pending from leave_requests. No-op for unlimited types. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.update`.

item.name: Leave Request
item.description: Per-employee leave application lifecycle (DRAFT → PENDING → APPROVED | REJECTED | CANCELLED). Employee endpoints (`/me`, create, cancel own) need only JWT; HR endpoints require `LEAVE_MGMT.{view|approve|delete}`.

request.description: Dry-run validation. Returns `is_valid`, computed days breakdown, balance_before/after — without persisting anything. Use this to power a 'Days: X' counter in the Apply Leave UI.

body.raw:
{
    "leave_type_id": "{{leave_type_id}}",
    "from_date": "2026-05-12",
    "to_date": "2026-05-14",
    "is_half_day": false,
    "reason": "Family vacation planned in advance.",
    "attachment_ids": [],
    "submit": true
}

request.description: Creates a leave request. With `submit: true` (default) it goes straight to PENDING and the balance is held. Set `submit: false` to save as DRAFT for editing later. The server computes `total_days` — never trust client-side computation.
Saves as DRAFT (no balance hold, no PENDING transition). Edit freely with PATCH, then call /submit when ready.
Caller's own requests. Includes `manager_note` per row (resolves to approver_comment / rejection_reason / admin cancel reason based on status).
Full detail view. Owner OR `LEAVE_MGMT.view`.
Append-only audit trail of state transitions (Applied → PENDING → APPROVED, etc.). Powers the request-detail timeline UI.
On PENDING only `reason` and `attachment_ids` are editable. To change dates, cancel and re-create. On DRAFT, all fields are editable.
On DRAFT: all fields editable. The server recomputes `total_days` and `computed_days_breakdown` on every PATCH.
Transitions DRAFT → PENDING. Re-runs all validations (overlap, balance, max-per-month, backdated).
Allowed if status is DRAFT, PENDING, or APPROVED (and from_date > today). Returns 422 ALREADY_CONSUMED otherwise.
Hard-deletes a DRAFT only. 422 INVALID_STATE for any other status.
HR list with full filters. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.view`.
All PENDING requests in the org that the caller is authorized to approve. v1: any user with `LEAVE_MGMT.approve` sees all pending requests in the org (manager-of routing is a v2 task).
PENDING → APPROVED. Self-approval forbidden. Re-validates balance to prevent stale-PENDING approvals. Atomic with row-lock on the balance row.
PENDING → REJECTED. Releases held days back to available. `rejection_reason` is mandatory.
HR can cancel any request — PENDING or APPROVED, even past start_date. Credits days back to balance. Requires `LEAVE_MGMT.delete`.

item.name: Attendance (Check-In / Check-Out)
item.description: Daily attendance flow: employees punch in/out from web or mobile (Office-In or Remote-In based on geo-fence), HR/admins monitor a real-time daily preview, post manual corrections, and resolve pending remote-attendance approvals. Requires the `ATTENDANCE` module/actions to be granted (see `docs/seeds/grant-module-access.sql`). Employee-facing endpoints only need a valid login.

item.name: Employee
item.description: Endpoints for the logged-in employee — no special permission needed beyond authentication.

request.description:
Punch in for the day from inside the office geo-fence.

**Validations**
- 409 if there is already an open session (`Employee already checked in`)
- 409 if a record already exists today and the rule does not allow multiple check-ins
- 400 `GPS_REQUIRED` if geo-fencing is enabled and lat/lng are missing
- Distance is computed by Haversine. Inside `office_radius_meters` ⇒ `Office-In`, outside ⇒ Remote-In flow

**Response data** includes `attendance_id`, `attendance_session_id`, `shift`, `office`, `is_late`. Auto-saves both ids to collection variables.

Punch in from outside the office radius (Remote-In). The body must include `remote_reason` if the rule has `require_remote_reason=true` (default).

**Errors**
- 400 `REMOTE_ATTENDANCE_DISABLED` if rule has `allow_remote_in=false`
- 400 `REMOTE_REASON_REQUIRED` if reason missing

If the rule has `remote_approval_required=true`, the record is created in status `PENDING_APPROVAL` and a row is added to `attendance_remote_approvals` for HR to review.

Close the active check-in session. Recomputes `worked_minutes`, `is_late`, `is_half_day`, `is_overtime`, and final `attendance_status` against the rule's `full_day_hours` / `half_day_hours` / `overtime_after_hours`.

Returns `worked_hours` as `HH:mm`. Returns 404 if there is no open session.

If the record is `PENDING_APPROVAL`, the status is left untouched until HR approves/rejects.

Drives the dashboard widget. Returns today's attendance record (or null fields if not yet checked in), the assigned shift, the office, and a live `worked_duration_live` (HH:mm:ss) computed against the open session, plus a `live_session` boolean.
Paginated personal attendance history with optional month/year/mode/status filters. Each row contains date, check-in/out (12-hour), worked hours, mode, status, shift name, and remote reason (if any).

Returns the attendance rule that applies to the logged-in employee (resolved from employee-level → department-level assignment). Includes `full_day_hours`, `half_day_hours`, `late_after`, `grace_minutes`, `overtime_after_hours`, `geo_fencing_enabled`, `office_radius_meters`, `allow_remote_in`, `require_remote_reason`, and `remote_approval_required`. Useful for the client to render the right CTA / location prompt before the user clicks Check-In.

item.name: Admin
item.description: HR / admin endpoints. All require `RequirePermission('ATTENDANCE', '<action>')` — view/create/update/delete/approve. Run `docs/seeds/grant-module-access.sql` (configured for the ATTENDANCE module) to grant access to a user.

request.description:
Backs the Admin → Daily Preview screen. Returns a `summary` block (total present, absent, late, half-day, on-leave, missed-checkout, office-in, remote-in) + paginated `items` with employee, shift, check-in/out times, worked hours, mode, status, geo info, source, remote reason and notes.

Requires `ATTENDANCE.view`.

Create an attendance record on behalf of an employee (forgot punch, server outage, biometric failure, etc.). Recomputes worked hours / status from the supplied check-in / check-out and the employee's effective rule.

If `attendance_status_override` is provided, it wins over the computed status. `source` is forced to `MANUAL`. Returns 409 if a record already exists for that employee on that date.

Requires `ATTENDANCE.create`. Auto-saves `attendance_id`.

body.raw:
{
  "employee_id": "{{employee_id}}",
  "date": "2026-05-12",
  "check_in": "09:12",
  "check_out": "18:05",
  "attendance_mode": "OFFICE_IN",
  "remote_reason": null,
  "notes": "Manual correction by HR — forgot to punch out"
}

request.description:
Update an existing attendance record (any combination of check-in, check-out, mode, status override, reason, notes). Recomputes worked hours / status from the new times and writes `STATUS_OVERRIDE` or `MANUAL_UPDATE` to the audit log.

Requires `ATTENDANCE.update`.
Soft-deletes the attendance record and removes its sessions. Writes `MANUAL_DELETE` to the audit log. Requires `ATTENDANCE.delete`.

Inbox of remote check-ins waiting on manager / HR action. Each item carries `attendance_id`, employee summary, `check_in_time`, `remote_reason`, and `distance_from_office_meters`. Requires `ATTENDANCE.view`.

Approves a `PENDING_APPROVAL` remote check-in. Recomputes the final attendance status from worked minutes (Present / Half-Day / Late / Absent) and stamps `REMOTE_APPROVE` in the audit log. Requires `ATTENDANCE.approve`.
Rejects a pending remote check-in. Forces the attendance status to `ABSENT` and records the rejection reason in the audit log. `reason` is mandatory. Requires `ATTENDANCE.approve`.

query.description: CHECK_IN | CHECK_OUT | OFFICE_IN | REMOTE_IN | GEO_REJECTION | MANUAL_CREATE | MANUAL_UPDATE | MANUAL_DELETE | STATUS_OVERRIDE | REMOTE_APPROVE | REMOTE_REJECT

request.description: Audit trail across all attendance events with filters by employee, date and event type. Returns the actor (`performed_by`), `device`, `ip_address`, and `old_value` / `new_value` JSON snapshots for each change. Requires `ATTENDANCE.view`.

item.name: Billing
item.description: Plan & Subscription experience: dashboard, plan comparison, upgrade/downgrade, invoices, Razorpay checkout, payment history, and GST billing profile. All endpoints require `JwtAuthGuard` except `webhooks/razorpay` (Razorpay-signed).

request.description: Single call powering the billing dashboard (screen 5.1). Returns: current subscription + plan + cycle + status, trial state (`is_trial`, `days_remaining`, `banner_level` 7/3/1), live employee billing (active count, price/employee, subtotal, GST 18%, estimated total), 5-step timeline (TRIAL_STARTED → TRIAL_ENDS → INVOICE_GENERATED → PAYMENT_DUE → SUBSCRIPTION_RENEWED), and `open_invoice_id` for the Pay Invoice CTA.

Plan comparison cards (screen 5.1 section 2). For each plan returns monthly + annual per-employee price, `annual_discount_percent`, estimated totals at current headcount, `is_current`, `is_current_cycle`, and the CTA label (`Current Plan` / `Upgrade Now` / `Downgrade Next Cycle` / `Switch to Annual` / `Switch to Monthly` / `Choose Plan`).

Upgrade or downgrade (screen 5.2). Server decides: tier upgrade / cycle upgrade (Monthly→Annual) / trial→paid → applied **immediate** and a prorated invoice is generated; tier or cycle downgrade → deferred to next renewal (`applied: 'next_cycle'`). Response: `{ subscription, invoice, applied }`.
Cancels a deferred downgrade (clears `pending_plan_id` / `pending_billing_cycle`). Subscription keeps current plan + cycle on next renewal. 400 if no pending change exists.
Cancels the active subscription immediately. `sub_status` flips to `Cancelled`, `end_date` set to now. Org becomes Expired-blocked after access expires.

Returns the org's open invoice — status in [`Pending`, `Failed`, `Overdue`]. Powers screen 5.3 (Current Invoice). Includes `line_items`, `subtotal`, `gst_rate`, `gst_amount`, `total`, and the frozen `billing_profile_snapshot`. 404 if nothing is open.
Paginated invoice history (screen 5.5). Returns `{ data, total, page, limit }` ordered by `invoice_date` DESC. Filter by status + date range.
Full invoice detail by ID — same shape as `current`. 404 if invoice is in a different org.

Returns `{ url, expires_in_seconds: 900 }` — a presigned S3 download URL. PDF is lazily generated (pdfkit) on first request and cached in S3. Layout follows section §8 of the design spec (header, line table, GST summary, footer).

Creates a Razorpay order for the invoice's `total` (in paise). Persists a `Payment` row with status `Initiated`. Response includes `razorpay_order_id`, `razorpay_key_id`, `amount` (paise), and `invoice_number` — hand these to the Razorpay JS checkout.

Verifies the HMAC signature returned by Razorpay's JS checkout. On success: payment → `Success`, invoice → `Paid`, subscription `end_date` extended by one billing cycle (+1mo or +12mo), `pending_plan_id` / `pending_billing_cycle` applied if set, `sub_status` → `Active`, grace cleared. Idempotent: replaying a successful verify returns the same state.
Polled by the 'Verifying your payment securely...' UI (screen 5.4) while the webhook lands. Returns the current `payment_status` (`Initiated` / `Processing` / `Success` / `Failed`).

header.description: HMAC SHA256 of the raw body using `RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Required.

request.description: **Public endpoint** — no JWT. Razorpay calls this on `payment.captured`, `payment.failed`, `order.paid`. Server verifies the `X-Razorpay-Signature` header against the raw body; mismatched signature → 401. Idempotent on `razorpay_payment_id`. Configure the URL + secret in your Razorpay dashboard.
Returns the org's `BillingProfile` (screen 5.6). Auto-creates on first read, seeded from `OrganizationProfile` (name, gst_no, address, etc.).

Upserts the GST-compliant billing profile. `gst_number` is auto-uppercased and validated against the 15-char GSTIN regex `^[0-9]{2}[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z][0-9][A-Z][0-9A-Z]$`. Pincode must be 6 digits. The profile is snapshotted into each future invoice's `billing_profile_snapshot`.
